sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Getafe are in poor form, winless in their last 5 matches with 3 losses and 2 draws, struggling to score and conceding frequently (6 goals against in last 5). Real Sociedad, on the other hand, show signs of improvement with no losses in their last 3 games (1 win, 2 draws), though they’ve also failed to keep clean sheets in their last 5 matches. This suggests a slight edge for Sociedad going into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Getafe have performed well, securing a 3-0 victory away at Real Sociedad in January 2025 and a 0-0 draw at home in September 2024. Getafe are unbeaten in their last 3 encounters against Sociedad, which could boost their confidence despite current form.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Getafe sit 10th in the league with 21 points, having scored just 14 goals (18th in La Liga) and conceded 23. Real Sociedad, 15th with 18 points, have a better attacking output with 22 goals but have also conceded 26. Sociedad’s superior goal-scoring ability and recent form slightly tilt the balance in their favor.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Getafe will miss key forward Borja Mayoral (4 goals) and several defenders, potentially weakening their attack and defense. Real Sociedad boast in-form players like Takefusa Kubo (7.54 average rating in last 5) and have no major absences, giving them a squad advantage.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Considering Sofascore’s prediction (21% home win, 34% draw, 45% away win) and current dynamics, Real Sociedad appear more likely to edge this contest due to Getafe’s poor form and missing players. However, Getafe’s recent head-to-head success and home advantage could make it a tight affair, with a draw also a plausible outcome.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Real Sociedad leads Getafe 1-0 in a match that has seen the away side dominate possession and create more entries into the final third. Brais Méndez scored the only goal in the 36th minute, capitalizing on a well-worked move to give Sociedad the edge. Getafe has struggled to convert their limited opportunities, while Sociedad appears more composed with the ball.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad has controlled the game with a significant advantage in ball possession and a higher number of final third entries and penalty area penetrations. Their aerial dominance and ability to win duels in key areas have kept Getafe on the back foot. Getafe, however, has shown resilience in defense despite the deficit, managing a reasonable number of clearances and maintaining a competitive duel win rate. Their attacking output remains limited, with fewer shots on target compared to their opponents.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Real Sociedad maintain consistent attacking pressure with several spells of dominance, particularly in the early stages and around the time of their goal. Getafe had moments of momentum, especially towards the end of the half, pushing forward with intent to level the score. However, Sociedad's ability to shift momentum back in their favor during critical periods has defined the flow of the game so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Real Sociedad, Brais Méndez has been the standout performer, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat in midfield. His composure and involvement in build-up play make him a pivotal figure. On the Getafe side, players like Mauro Arambarri and Juan Iglesias have shown glimpses of intent with their shots on target, and they could be crucial in sparking a comeback if given space in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Getafe is expected to come out with more urgency in the second half, likely pushing for an equalizer by committing more players forward. This could open up spaces for Real Sociedad to exploit on the counter, given their superior possession and final third presence. Tactical adjustments from Getafe’s coaching staff may focus on increasing their attacking output, while Sociedad will aim to manage the game and protect their lead. A closely contested half is on the cards, with both teams having opportunities to score.
